---
title: "pleasant activities reading and plan"
format: html
---

## Pleasant Activities Reading and Plan

Early recovery can feel flat while your reward system recalibrates. Pleasant and meaningful activities help rebuild natural reward, reduce boredom, and lower relapse vulnerability.

The target is consistency, not intensity. Small, repeatable actions are often more effective than occasional major efforts.

Mood and motivation often fluctuate during recovery. Low energy, anhedonia, and withdrawal can make alcohol seem like quick relief, especially when routines become unstructured.

Behavioral activation principles show that consistent, meaningful action often improves mood more reliably than waiting to feel ready. Building a realistic activity plan restores momentum, reward, and resilience over time.

::: callout
### Reflection Questions
1. Which activities improve my mood without increasing risk?
2. What gets in the way of doing them?
3. What is one low-effort activity I can do daily?
:::

| Activity | Day/Time | With whom | Mood before (0-10) | Mood after (0-10) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| | | | | |
| | | | | |
| | | | | |
| | | | | |
